Findings.Title: Microsoft Defender Protection Disabled
Findings.Description: Real-time protection: False; Antivirus enabled: True. At least one core protection feature is off.
Findings.Remediation: Enable real-time protection and ensure Defender (or managed AV) is active. In Group Policy: Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Windows Components > Microsoft Defender Antivirus.

Findings.Title: Remote Desktop Disabled
Findings.Description: Remote Desktop connections are denied by policy (fDenyTSConnections).
Findings.Remediation: No action required for NLA; RDP is off.

Findings.Title: SMB1 Protocol Not Enabled
Findings.Description: SMB1 optional feature is not enabled (Disabled or Absent).

Findings.Title: BitLocker Protection
Findings.Description: Listed mount point(s) appear protected by BitLocker per policy.bitLocker.mountPoints.

Findings.Title: WinRM Service Not Running
Findings.Description: WinRM service is stopped (not listening for remote management by default).
Findings.Remediation: No action required unless you intend to enable remote PowerShell/WinRM.

Findings.Title: High-Risk Port Open: 445
Findings.Description: Port 445 is listening on address(es): ::. Process(es): System (PID 4). Primary executable: . Signature: N/A. Merged 1 listener row(s) (e.g. IPv4/IPv6). At least one listener matched policy allowlist.
Findings.Remediation: Restrict access with firewall rules or disable unnecessary service. Verify the owning process and binary signature.

Findings.Title: High-Risk Port Open: 135
Findings.Description: Port 135 is listening on address(es): ::, 0.0.0.0. Process(es): svchost (PID 1500). Primary executable: C:\WINDOWS\system32\svchost.exe. Signature: Valid. Merged 2 listener row(s) (e.g. IPv4/IPv6). At least one listener matched Windows system-binary downgrade rules.
Findings.Remediation: Restrict access with firewall rules or disable unnecessary service. Verify the owning process and binary signature.

Findings.Title: High-Risk Port Open: 139
Findings.Description: Port 139 is listening on address(es): 192.168.1.18. Process(es): System (PID 4). Primary executable: . Signature: N/A. Merged 1 listener row(s) (e.g. IPv4/IPv6).
Findings.Remediation: Restrict access with firewall rules or disable unnecessary service. Verify the owning process and binary signature.

Findings.Title: Auto-Start Services Path Review
Findings.Description: No suspicious auto-start service paths detected.

Findings.Title: Startup File Hashing
Findings.Description: No executable files found in startup folders.

Findings.Title: Suspicious Scheduled Tasks Found
Findings.Description:
3 scheduled task action(s) execute from user-writable paths.

Details:
- \BraveSoftwareUpdateTaskUserS-1-5-21-4235219758-1704497576-1465918199-1001Core{4A8A9D58-0C0B-4984-B576-4190C75A302E} | Run: "C:\Users\moath\AppData\Local\BraveSoftware\Update\BraveUpdate.exe" | Args: /c | RunAs: moath
- \BraveSoftwareUpdateTaskUserS-1-5-21-4235219758-1704497576-1465918199-1001UA{2B58177E-B669-4A3E-B5AF-FBB40D37204E} | Run: "C:\Users\moath\AppData\Local\BraveSoftware\Update\BraveUpdate.exe" | Args: /ua /installsource scheduler | RunAs: moath
- \ZoomUpdateTaskUser-S-1-5-21-4235219758-1704497576-1465918199-1001 | Run: "C:\Users\moath\AppData\Roaming\Zoom\bin\Zoom.exe" | Args: --action=UpdateSchedule | RunAs: moath
Findings.Remediation: Disable unknown tasks and verify binary source/trust. Open Task Scheduler and review the task paths listed above.

Findings.Title: Suspicious Run / RunOnce Entries
Findings.Description:
4 Run/RunOnce value(s) reference suspicious paths.

Details:
- [HKCU\Run] Discord = "C:\Users\moath\AppData\Local\Discord\Update.exe" --processStart Discord.exe
- [HKCU\Run] BraveSoftware Update = "C:\Users\moath\AppData\Local\BraveSoftware\Update\1.3.361.151\BraveUpdateCore.exe"
- [HKCU\Run] CiscoMeetingDaemon = "C:\Users\moath\AppData\Local\WebEx\WebexHost.exe" /daemon /runFrom=autorun
- [HKCU\Run] Teams = "C:\Users\moath\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps\MSTeams_8wekyb3d8bbwe\ms-teams.exe" msteams:system-initiated
Findings.Remediation: Remove unknown persistence from registry Run keys or validate binaries. Compare with autoruns baselines.

Findings.Title: WMI Event Subscription Consumers
Findings.Description: No WMI event consumers in root\subscription reference suspicious user/temp paths.

Findings.Title: Active Network Connections Snapshot
Findings.Description: Captured a snapshot of active TCP connections (limited to 500 rows). Established sessions to non-local addresses: 36
Findings.Remediation: Review unknown remote endpoints and investigate corresponding process IDs.
